The framework's only first-class plugin-authoring path is `netscript plugin new` (dual-tier core+connector package, proxy/feature kind) — heavyweight machinery meant for gateway/service-surfaced plugins. channel-sync's actual need (a single backend-swappable domain interface, no service/gateway surface, no lifecycle) has no named lighter-weight seam, so the app fell back to bare foldering under `paths.plugins`. A documented 'interface + adapter, no plugin package needed' composition pattern (or a lighter scaffold tier) would remove the ambiguity between 'this lives in plugins/ because it's a NetScript plugin' vs 'this lives in plugins/ because the folder name matched.'

JobBuilder.retry(maxRetries, { backoff, delayMs, maxDelayMs }) accepts a backoff strategy but the runtime never consumes it — retries are always a flat constant delay. issue_candidates: FA1/FA2 hardcode the `/ai/chat` durable-stream subpath with no override hook. Evidence: `NETSCRIPT_CHAT_STREAM_SUBPATH = '/ai/chat'` in packages/fresh/src/runtime/ai/create-chat-connection.ts (netscript-framework) is a fixed constant, not a configurable option on `NetScriptChatSessionTarget`/`NetScriptChatConnectionOptions`; eis-chat's own convention is `/eischat/sessions/{id}/messages` (apps/dashboard/lib/chat-stream.ts:17-19), which is incompatible with the hardcoded path and is the concrete reason eis-chat re-implemented FA1/FA2's whole composition independently instead of importing it. Fix: accept an optional path-builder/prefix on the options bag.

FA2 (`createNetScriptChatStreamProxy`) may not fence the crash flavor of netscript#239/#219 it claims to fix. Evidence: FA2's fix (packages/fresh/src/runtime/ai/stream-proxy.ts `sanitizeUpstreamResponse`) strips response headers AFTER `await doFetch(upstreamRequest)` resolves, premised on the body having already been 'decoded and re-framed' successfully; but eis-chat's own workaround comments on the SAME durable-chat-session domain (apps/dashboard/routes/api/chat-stream.ts:65-72; apps/dashboard/lib/stream-loaders.ts:78-82; apps/dashboard/routes/api/streams/[...path].ts:12-21) describe the auto-decompressing `fetch` THROWING `Invalid gzip header` — a decode-time crash, not a stale-header symptom — which post-hoc header stripping cannot repair since the mislabeled bytes are consumed by the decompression transform before FA2's handler ever sees a usable response. FA2 never sets `Accept-Encoding: identity` or otherwise disables decompression on the upstream request. Recommend verifying FA2 against the actual mislabeling runtime bug (not just a synthetic stale-header test) before treating it as sufficient to close #219's identity-workaround-removal criterion.
